name: Gerald Wiggins
abstract: Gerald Foster Wiggins (May 12, 1922 – July 13, 2008) was an American jazz pianist and organist.

interviews.title: Gerald Wiggins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Los Angeles, California, September 2, 1995

concerts.label: JVC Jazz Festival New York: Gerry Wiggins

works.t: The New Yorkers: Love for Sale

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: I worked with Gerry Wiggins and Bill Douglass in a place called the Turban Room... It was Gerry Wiggins' trio.

about_quotes.speaker: Joe Wilder Part 1
about_quotes.evidence: The first big band I played with... was Les Hite... Gerald Wiggins joined the band. He was in the band at the time Dizzy Gillespie was in the band for a short time.
about_quotes.doc_title: Joe Wilder Part 1 interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996

about_quotes.speaker: Chico Hamilton
about_quotes.evidence: So me, and Klook, and Gerry Wiggins we’re hanging out... So man they were in bad shape. They didn’t have any money so me and Wig did a record.
about_quotes.doc_title: Chico Hamilton — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Leonidas Raymond Young
about_quotes.evidence: I think at this time, it seems to me that I had ... Gerald Wiggins ... playing in this band with me.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Callender
about_quotes.evidence: here lately, I have had and have the pleasure of working with Gerald Wiggins

about_quotes.speaker: Pierre Boussaguet
about_quotes.evidence: That was a record under the name of Sweets, a quintet with Eddie “Lockjaw” Davis, Oliver Jackson drums, beautiful piano player Gerry Wiggins, and Major Holley on bass.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pierre Boussaguet interviewed by Matthieu Jaffre, Aix en Provence, France, July 28, 2020
